problem: Every options-MC artifact must carry immutable provenance fields (`generated_at`, `config_hash`, batch/path consistency metadata). Current artifacts are inconsistent (e.g., null generated_at in some files).

evidence.symptom: Operator trust and auditability degrade when source/config cannot be cryptographically tied to result.

required_change: In `scripts/ak_options_mc.py`, write mandatory fields into every JSON artifact: `generated_at`, `config_hash`, `n_batches`, `paths_per_batch`, `n_total_paths`, `assumptions.n_paths`, `base_seed`, `crn_scope`.
Define `config_hash` as deterministic hash over run-critical config (model, structure, risk/friction params, seeds, path counts).
Add schema validation in producer and fail artifact write if mandatory provenance keys are missing.
Update `scripts/mc_command.py` to hard-fail if any mandatory provenance field is absent or invalid.
acceptance_tests: New test: generated options-MC JSON always contains mandatory provenance keys and non-null `generated_at`.
New test: `mc_command.py --json` fails closed when provenance keys are missing/malformed.
Manual proof: provide one fresh `kb/experiments/options-mc-*.json` sample showing full provenance populated.
constraints: No silent fallback for missing provenance; must fail closed.
Do not alter risk gate thresholds in this ticket.
Preserve existing trace IDs and JSON stability where possible.
